name: git-stacky

metadata.name: git_stacky
metadata.summary: Minimal Git extension for working with multi-commit stacks

documentation:
# Stacky

Stacky is a minimal Git extension providing a [stack-like](https://www.stacking.dev/) workflow with one PR per stack, rather than one PR per commit. This allows for easier integration with GitHub's current PR workflow.

Since GitHub doesn't natively support stacked PRs, [workarounds](https://github.com/gitext-rs/git-stack/blob/main/docs/comparison.md) fall into one of three categories: serial PRs (simple but slow), parallel PRs (fast but complex and requiring permissions), or single PRs with multiple commits (fast but less flexible to review). Stacky implements the latter, with a simple and intuitive interface.

## Concept

The main idea is every *stack* is just a branch `FEATURE` with linear history rooted at its base branch `FEATURE_base`

```bash
(main)(FEATURE_base) > commit1 > commit2 > ... > commitN (FEATURE)
```

Successive commits move the stack's top branch, and rebases move the top-to-bottom stack of changes onto a new location.

One PR is created per stack, allowing reviewers to go through changes one commit at a time, while still testing and merging the stack as a single change.

Stacky is small and easy to understand, integrates natively with GitHub, and requires zero repo or org-level admin permissions or changes.

## Install

```bash
brew install git-absorb  # optional
pip install git_stacky
```

## Usage

Stacky provides four commands to manage stacks:
- `hack FEATURE` create a new stack named `FEATURE`
- `rebase TARGET` rebase the current stack onto `TARGET`
- `stacks` list all stacks, optionally as a `--graph`
- `absorb` [absorb](https://github.com/tummychow/git-absorb) changes into the current stack

Here's an example workflow:

```bash
# Help text
(main)   git stack -h

# 1. New stack (apple, apple_base branches rooted at main)
(main)   git stack hack apple
(apple)  touch apple1 && git add -A && git commit -m 'apple1'
(apple)  touch apple2 && git add -A && git commit -m 'apple2'

# 2. New stack (banana, banana_base branches rooted at main)
(apple)  git stack hack banana
(banana) touch banana1 && git add -A && git commit -m 'banana1'

# 3. Rebase banana onto apple
(banana) git stack rebase apple

# 4. Rebase banana implicitly back onto main
(banana) git stack rebase

# 5. Force-delete banana stack
(banana) git checkout apple
(apple)  git stack stacks -D banana

# History evolution
# 1. (main)(apple_base) > apple1 > apple2 (apple)
# 2. (main)(apple_base)(banana_base) > apple1 > apple2 (apple)
#                                    > banana1 (banana)
# 3. (main)(apple_base) > apple1 > apple2 (apple)(banana_base) > banana1 (banana)
# 4. (main)(apple_base)(banana_base) > apple1 > apple2 (apple)
#                                    > banana1 (banana)
# 5. (main)(apple_base) > apple1 > apple2 (apple)
```

modules.name: git_stacky.cli
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.hack
documentation:
Update main and optionally create a new stack.

 
Function:
  hack        => update main, stay on starting branch
  hack TARGET => update main, create TARGET from main if it doesn't exist, and switch to it

 
Examples:
  (main)    hack          => update main
  (main)    hack FEATURE  => update main, create+switch to FEATURE
  (FEATURE) hack          => update main
  (FEATURE) hack main     => update main, switch to main

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.rebase
documentation:
Rebase current stack onto main or the specified target.

 
Examples:
  (FEATURE) rebase        => rebase FEATURE onto main
  (FEATURE) rebase TARGET => rebase FEATURE onto TARGET
  (FEATURE) rebase --done => finish rebasing FEATURE, if original rebase was interrupted

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.stacks
documentation:
Manage and visualize stacks.

 
Examples:
  stacks                     => list all stacks
  stacks --graph             => graph all stacks
  stacks --delete FEATURE    => delete FEATURE stack

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.absorb
documentation:
Automatically absorb changes into the stack.

Requires git-absorb to be installed.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.rebase_only
documentation: Rebase a stack onto the target.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.rebase_done
documentation: Finish rebasing a stack.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.save_rebase_args
documentation: Save null-separated rebase arguments to ~/.stacky/rebase_args.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.load_rebase_args
documentation: Load null-separated rebase arguments from ~/.stacky/rebase_args.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.clear_rebase_args
documentation: Clear rebase arguments.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.delete_stacks_all
documentation: Delete all stacks.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.delete_stacks
documentation: Delete stacks.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.list_stacks
documentation: List all tracked stacks.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.get_stacks
documentation: Return a list of tracked stacks.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.get_branches
documentation: Return a list of branches.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.main_branch_name
documentation: Return the name of the main branch.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.workflow_type
documentation: Return the type of Git workflow.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.branch_exists
documentation: Check if a branch exists.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.current_branch
documentation: Return the name of the current branch, or an empty string if detached.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.validate_branches
documentation: Validate branch names.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.validate_target_hack_branch
documentation: Validate the target branch for the hack command. Returns True iff the target needs to be created.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.strip_suffix
documentation: Strip a suffix from a string.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.run
documentation: Run a shell command and return the output.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.must_run
documentation: Run a shell command and return the output, or exit on error.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.try_run
documentation: Run a shell command and return the output, or return None on error.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.cexit
documentation: Print an error message and exit.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.log
documentation: Print a message.

functions.name: git_stacky.cli.fmt_log
documentation: Capitalize the first letter of a log message.
